methods.description: Procedure for preparing an undisturbed soil sample and saturating it with artificial seawater through percolation.

steps.description: The original soil sample measuring 60 mm in diameter and 20 mm in height was prepared by trimming the undisturbed soil sample that was extruded from the thin-walled tube.
steps.searchable_summary: trim undisturbed soil sample to specified dimensions
inputs: undisturbed soil sample (extruded from thin-walled tube)
outputs: trimmed soil sample (60 mm diameter, 20 mm height)

steps.description: The trimmed soil sample was placed in a fixed consolidation ring made of brass, measuring 60 mm in diameter and 20 mm in height, and allowed to consolidate under a vertical pressure equivalent to the effective overburden pressure on the soil sample in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: place trimmed soil sample in consolidation ring and apply vertical pressure
inputs: fixed consolidation ring (brass, 60 mm diameter, 20 mm height)
vertical pressure (equivalent to effective overburden pressure)
outputs: soil sample in consolidation ring under pressure

steps.description: This continued until primary consolidation was completed.
steps.searchable_summary: continue consolidation until primary consolidation is complete
inputs: soil sample in consolidation ring under pressure

steps.description: Artificial seawater with a typical ion composition (Table 1) was prepared using commercially available seawater powder (Marine Art SF-1, Tomita Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare artificial seawater solution

steps.description: The artificial seawater was introduced from a standpipe with inside diameter of 1.5 cm into porous stone with a pore size of 0.18 mm; the water was allowed to percolate through the original soil sample (Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: set up percolation system to introduce artificial seawater through porous stone into soil sample

steps.description: The artificial seawater was left to percolate for one week, until the salinity of leachate from the original soil sample was identical to that of the artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: continue seawater percolation for one week and verify leachate salinity matches artificial seawater

methods.description: Procedure for preparing clay fractions from soil samples and analyzing their mineral composition using X-ray diffraction after various chemical and heat treatments.

steps.description: Original soil sample suspensions for X-ray diffraction analysis were treated with 7% H 2 O 2 to remove organic matter, deflocculated by sonification, and then adjusted to a pH of 10 with 1 M NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: treat soil suspension with H2O2 to remove organic matter
outputs: soil suspension with organic matter removed

steps.description: Original soil sample suspensions for X-ray diffraction analysis were treated with 7% H 2 O 2 to remove organic matter, deflocculated by sonification, and then adjusted to a pH of 10 with 1 M NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: deflocculate soil suspension by sonification
inputs: soil suspension with organic matter removed

steps.description: Original soil sample suspensions for X-ray diffraction analysis were treated with 7% H 2 O 2 to remove organic matter, deflocculated by sonification, and then adjusted to a pH of 10 with 1 M NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust pH of soil suspension to 10 using NaOH

steps.description: The <2 μm clay fractions were then siphoned from the soil suspension and collected.
steps.searchable_summary: siphon less than 2 micrometer clay fractions from soil suspension

steps.description: The <2 μm clay fractions were then siphoned from the soil suspension and collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect less than 2 micrometer clay fractions

steps.description: Duplicate clay suspensions were prepared, each containing 50 mg of clay fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare duplicate clay suspensions with 50 mg clay fractions each
outputs: duplicate clay suspensions (50 mg clay each)

steps.description: One was saturated with Mg 2+ by washing with 0.5 M MgCl 2 ; the other was saturated with K + using 1 M KCl.
steps.searchable_summary: saturate one clay suspension with Mg2+ using MgCl2

steps.description: One was saturated with Mg 2+ by washing with 0.5 M MgCl 2 ; the other was saturated with K + using 1 M KCl.
steps.searchable_summary: saturate the other clay suspension with K+ using KCl

steps.description: Excess salt was then removed by washing with water.
steps.searchable_summary: wash saturated clay suspensions with water to remove excess salt

steps.description: One cubic centimeter of water, together with an aliquot of the suspension containing 30 mg of clay fractions, was dropped onto a glass slide (28 × 48 mm), air-dried, and then X-rayed.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare glass slides by dropping clay suspension aliquot (30 mg clay) with water
outputs: glass slides with clay suspension aliquot

steps.description: One cubic centimeter of water, together with an aliquot of the suspension containing 30 mg of clay fractions, was dropped onto a glass slide (28 × 48 mm), air-dried, and then X-rayed.
steps.searchable_summary: air-dry prepared glass slides
inputs: glass slides with clay suspension aliquot

steps.description: One cubic centimeter of water, together with an aliquot of the suspension containing 30 mg of clay fractions, was dropped onto a glass slide (28 × 48 mm), air-dried, and then X-rayed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction on air-dried slides

steps.description: The K-saturated specimens were heated first at 300˚C and then at 550˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: heat K-saturated specimens at 300 degrees C

steps.description: The K-saturated specimens were heated first at 300˚C and then at 550˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: heat K-saturated specimens at 550 degrees C

steps.description: The Mg-saturated specimens were solvated with glycerol to help identify smectite.
steps.searchable_summary: solvate Mg-saturated specimens with glycerol
outputs: Mg-saturated glass slides solvated with glycerol

steps.description: The treated specimens were subjected to a Rigaku diffractometer with Ni-filtered CuKα radiation at 40 kV and 20 mA, with a scanning speed of 2˚ 2θ mm −1 , a scanning step of 0.02˚, and a scanning mode in a range of 3˚ to 30˚ 2θ.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction on heat-treated K-saturated and glycerol-solvated Mg-saturated specimens
inputs: Mg-saturated glass slides solvated with glycerol
XRD parameters (2˚ 2θ mm−1 scan speed, 0.02˚ step, 3˚-30˚ 2θ range)

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and analyzing the chemical composition (EC, ions, pH, salinity) of pore water from soil samples (leachate and squeezed solutions).

steps.description: The pore water chemistry was determined using the leachate and the squeezed solutions from the original and seawater-saturated soil samples.The leachate was collected every 12 h during leaching.
steps.searchable_summary: collect leachate samples every 12 hours during soil leaching

steps.description: For squeezed solutions, a 50 g soil sample before and after leaching was placed in a separatory funnel and pore water was squeezed through a mini-pore filter with a pore size of 1.0 µm using vacuum filtration.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare soil sample (50g) in separatory funnel for pore water squeezing

steps.description: For squeezed solutions, a 50 g soil sample before and after leaching was placed in a separatory funnel and pore water was squeezed through a mini-pore filter with a pore size of 1.0 µm using vacuum filtration.
steps.searchable_summary: squeeze pore water from soil samples using vacuum filtration and mini-pore filter

steps.description: Electric conductivity (EC), the concentrations of Na + , Ca 2+ , and K + , and pH for both the leachate and squeezed solutions were determined using LAQUA Twin meters (Horiba Co. Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: measure electric conductivity (EC) of leachate and squeezed solutions

steps.description: Electric conductivity (EC), the concentrations of Na + , Ca 2+ , and K + , and pH for both the leachate and squeezed solutions were determined using LAQUA Twin meters (Horiba Co. Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: measure concentrations of Na+, Ca2+, and K+ in leachate and squeezed solutions

steps.description: Electric conductivity (EC), the concentrations of Na + , Ca 2+ , and K + , and pH for both the leachate and squeezed solutions were determined using LAQUA Twin meters (Horiba Co. Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH of leachate and squeezed solutions

steps.description: The salinity (mg/L) of the soil sample solutions was calculated by multiplying 640 by EC (mS/cm) [14], which was regarded as pore water salinity.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate salinity from EC values using conversion factor 640

steps.description: The concentrations of Cl − and 2 4 SO − were determined using a portable water quality analyzer (pHotoFlexTurb, Merck, Central Kagaku, Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: measure concentrations of Cl- and SO42- in leachate and squeezed solutions

steps.description: The concentration of Mg 2+ was determined by means of an atomic absorption spectrophotometer (AAS-Solar 52 Thermo Electronic Cooperation).
steps.searchable_summary: measure concentration of Mg2+ in leachate and squeezed solutions using atomic absorption spectrophotometry

methods.description: Procedure for determining the particle size distribution of an original soil sample using the hydrometer method.

steps.description: Fifteen grams of original air-dried soil sample was mixed with 250 mL deionized water in a 500 mL beaker, and 100 mL 6% H 2 O 2 was added to remove organic matter from the soil sample with addition of 1 M NaOH, and soil suspension was then subjected to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: mix air-dried soil sample with deionized water

steps.description: Fifteen grams of original air-dried soil sample was mixed with 250 mL deionized water in a 500 mL beaker, and 100 mL 6% H 2 O 2 was added to remove organic matter from the soil sample with addition of 1 M NaOH, and soil suspension was then subjected to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: add hydrogen peroxide to soil mixture to remove organic matter

steps.description: Fifteen grams of original air-dried soil sample was mixed with 250 mL deionized water in a 500 mL beaker, and 100 mL 6% H 2 O 2 was added to remove organic matter from the soil sample with addition of 1 M NaOH, and soil suspension was then subjected to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: add NaOH to soil mixture

steps.description: Fifteen grams of original air-dried soil sample was mixed with 250 mL deionized water in a 500 mL beaker, and 100 mL 6% H 2 O 2 was added to remove organic matter from the soil sample with addition of 1 M NaOH, and soil suspension was then subjected to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform particle size analysis using hydrometer method on soil suspension

methods.description: Procedure for determining the liquid limit of soil samples using a standard liquid limit device.

steps.description: The liquid limit was determined by measuring the water content at the number of 25 blows required to close a specific width groove for a specified length in a standard liquid limit device.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water content at liquid limit using standard liquid limit device

methods.description: Procedure for determining the plastic limit of soil samples.

steps.description: The plastic limit was determined by measuring the water content of the soil samples when threads of the soil sample 3 mm in diameter began to crumble.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water content at plastic limit when soil threads crumble

methods.method_name: Undisturbed Shear Strength (Consolidated Constant-Volume Box-Shear Test)
methods.description: Procedure for determining the shear strength of undisturbed soil samples using a consolidated constant-volume box-shear test.

steps.description: Before and after leaching, the soil sample measuring 60 mm in diameter and 20 mm in height was placed in a shear box and consolidated under a vertical pressure of 24.9 to 27.0 kPa, equivalent to the effective overburden pressure on the soil sample in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: place undisturbed soil sample in shear box
inputs: undisturbed soil sample (60 mm diameter, 20 mm height, before leaching)
undisturbed soil sample (60 mm diameter, 20 mm height, after leaching)

steps.description: Before and after leaching, the soil sample measuring 60 mm in diameter and 20 mm in height was placed in a shear box and consolidated under a vertical pressure of 24.9 to 27.0 kPa, equivalent to the effective overburden pressure on the soil sample in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: consolidate soil sample in shear box under specified vertical pressure
outputs: consolidating soil sample in shear box

steps.description: After completion of primary consolidation, the soil samples were subjected to shearing.
steps.searchable_summary: wait for completion of primary consolidation
inputs: consolidating soil sample in shear box
outputs: consolidated soil sample ready for shearing

steps.description: After completion of primary consolidation, the soil samples were subjected to shearing.
steps.searchable_summary: perform shearing on consolidated soil sample in box shear test
inputs: consolidated soil sample in shear box

methods.description: Procedure for determining the shear strength of remolded soil samples using a vane shear apparatus.

steps.description: First, the soil sample was remolded thoroughly by hand; then, it was placed in a container made of vinyl chloride measuring 31 mm in diameter and 50 mm in height.
steps.searchable_summary: remold soil sample thoroughly by hand

steps.description: First, the soil sample was remolded thoroughly by hand; then, it was placed in a container made of vinyl chloride measuring 31 mm in diameter and 50 mm in height.
steps.searchable_summary: place remolded soil sample in container
inputs: container (vinyl chloride, 31 mm diameter, 50 mm height)

steps.description: The vane was then gently inserted into the remolded soil sample, until the top of the vane reached under the surface of the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: gently insert vane into remolded soil sample
outputs: remolded soil sample with vane inserted

steps.description: The vane was then rotated at a speed of 6 degrees per minute, until the shear stress reached a constant value.
steps.searchable_summary: rotate vane at specified speed and measure torque
inputs: remolded soil sample with vane inserted

steps.description: The vane was then rotated at a speed of 6 degrees per minute, until the shear stress reached a constant value.
steps.searchable_summary: identify maximum torque or constant shear stress value from torque data

steps.description: The remolded strength (peak shear stress) was computed from Equation ( 2), assuming that the soil sample was isotropic in strength [15].
steps.searchable_summary: compute remolded shear strength from maximum torque, vane width, and vane height using vane shear formula

steps.description: The remolded strength (peak shear stress) was computed from Equation ( 2), assuming that the soil sample was isotropic in strength [15].
steps.searchable_summary: assume soil sample is isotropic in strength for calculation
outputs: remolded shear strength interpreted as isotropic
